title: gnidia bakeri
intro: Gnidia bakeri is a plant species commonly found in South Africa, belonging to the family Thymelaeaceae, and is also known as the "red poison" due to its toxic properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Gnidia bakeri include its potential use in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Herbal preparations of Gnidia bakeri are often made by infusing the plant's leaves or roots in water, but due to its toxic nature, caution is advised when handling the plant and using its preparations.
